question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookStorageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdAndKeyInAndDeletedIsFalse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HookStoreTransactionBody with a crafted key that, after DomainUtils.leftPadBytes() normalization to 32 bytes, collides with another account's existing storage key under the same hookId, causing the mirror node to overwrite the victim's hook storage entry and return corrupted data to legitimate queries?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookStorageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdAndKeyBetweenAndDeletedIsFalse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a key whose raw bytes, when compared using Arrays.compareUnsigned() in HookStorageRequest.getKeysInRange(), fall outside the declared range but whose left-padded form falls inside the database range used by findByOwnerIdAndHookIdAndKeyBetweenAndDeletedIsFalse(), causing a semantic mismatch that leaks or injects unexpected storage entries?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookStorageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdAndKeyBetweenAndDeletedIsFalse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted mapping slot preimage that, after keccak256(keccak256(preimage), leftPadBytes(mappingSlot, 32)) derivation, produces a key that collides with an existing storage slot of a victim's hook, causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdAndKeyBetweenAndDeletedIsFalse() to return the attacker's injected value instead of the victim's original value?
